reviews.review_description: Its pretty cool and is a nod to a game I really enjoyed on the phone around 15 years ago where you combine elements to make new elements but now with a modern VR twist. The elemental crafting system is not just intuitive—it’s addictive! Mixing fire, water, earth, and air feels both natural and surprising, and the satisfaction of discovering new combinations never gets old.  I love it when you get the click of a new element and the world in front opens up with more animations and things to look at as you progress in the game.  I could not find a save and exit button so feel that would be a great addition rather than using the Meta button to exit the game.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for playing and for leaving such a thoughtful review, it really means a lot!

reviews.review_description: Forge of Element is a surprisingly engaging and clever puzzle game. I love how it lets you start with the four basic elements and gradually combine them into more complex creations. Finding the right combinations feels like a genuine brain-teaser, and it’s incredibly satisfying when everything clicks. The visuals are warm and charming, giving the whole experience a friendly atmosphere. I also really appreciate the mixed-reality option, which adds an extra layer of immersion. On top of that, the game is easy to control even without a controller, making it very accessible. Overall, it’s a well-crafted and addictive puzzle experience that keeps you thinking and experimenting.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the kind words! I’m really glad the element combining puzzles clicked for you, and that the mixed-reality mode and hand tracking added to the experience. Appreciate you taking the time to share this feedback!

reviews.review_description: There is an error with the carpentry and metal merge. Other that that it's a really good game

developer_response.body: Thanks for the heads up! I’ll take a look at the carpentry + metal merge bug. Glad to hear you are enjoying it overall

reviews.review_description:
A god like, mix the elements, creation game.

You get out straight into the game, no menu, just a table in front of you in MR, and have to figure stuff out yourself.. no handholding here.
Don’t seem to be any story to the game, but there is missions kind of that show in the “Realms” button… but only by the names of each, which give idea of what the aim is.
You have to choose the elements and mix n match them together to find new elements, placing them on the table to make things appear. 
There is a bubble to show what you are needed to create next to advance the Realm you are currently working on.

Would be good to be able to place the element bubbles where you like while you try to mix them… there is areas to place them but they spring back to the centre if not placed in exact areas around you.

Game is interested with seeing how each area advances and the satisfaction of getting elements correct to make a new one.

There is an Almanac that shows various ingredients to examine and discover.

May appeal to puzzlers… or something easier to chill to, but can get frustrating with trying elements over and over to get the one you are needing.

Graphics are ok and the sounds are basic but do their job, with a tune playing in the background. Maybe more tunes would add variety too

developer_response.body: Thanks for the detailed feedback, really appreciate you taking the time to write this. The hands on elemental puzzling was a deliberate choice, and the notes on bubble placement and music variety are great feedback and noted for future improvements.

reviews.review_description: You begin with four basic elements (fire, water, earth, air) and your task is to combine them to discover new elements, transform your environment, and gradually shape “vibrant worlds.” The pace is tranquil, more meditative than action packed. It emphasizes exploration, and experimentation, .  Do pick it up if you enjoy slower, exploratory puzzle/creation games in VR, and like letting the experience evolve at your pace.

developer_response.body: Thank you for playing and sharing your experience. I’m glad you enjoyed the calming, exploratory side of the game!

reviews.review_description: I don't always follow the expected rules when opening an app or game and this was such an occasion.  I thought I knew what to do, so I just started combining elements.  I didn't read the instruction in front of me until I was running out of ideas.  i thought it was odd that water + earth didn't make mud until I realized maybe I needed to follow the instruction to continue, so I tried that.  Unfortunately, when the instructions said to combine water and earth to make mud, it still wouldn't work.  I thought maybe there might be a way to start over, but didn't find anything.  Sounds like you need to either enforce the instructions or allow people to skip them.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the honest feedback, really appreciate it!

You’re right, that combo absolutely should have worked, regardless of whether the tutorial was followed or not. I’ve been digging into it and found a bug that was causing the interaction to silently fail in some cases. A fix is already in place and will be out in the next update.

Thanks again for playing and for taking the time to write this up. Super helpful as I keep improving the experience!

reviews.review_description:
Forge of elements is like mix and match maths game.
Fun to play, picking an element and adding another to the equation to produce another, simple but very clever.
Great mixed Reality and easy to pick up.
Nice.

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing and for the kind review. I’m glad you enjoyed the elemental mix-and-match fun!

reviews.review_description: Very fun game, lots of potential for other worlds and elements to play around with.

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing and for the kind words. I’m glad you had fun, and I’m excited to keep adding more worlds and elements!

reviews.review_description: For early access, it looks pretty cool. There is nice potential for many levels. There are tons of combinations with elements, which makes it pretty cool.

developer_response.body: Thank you for playing in early access and sharing your feedback. I really appreciate the support as I keep building and adding more!
